page: Amazon Web Services r7i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong single-core performance, scoring in the top 25% for Geekbench single-core, image processing, and text processing. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng and Passmark CPU tests, falls within the average range.

The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers strong write performance for large blocks, placing in the top 25% of benchmarks, while read bandwidth remains in the average tier. This memory profile supports efficient data processing, as evidenced by top-tier performance in small-model LLM text generation and strong static web serving throughput. However, for larger workloads such as 70B parameter LLM text generation, performance drops to the bottom 25% due to the lack of GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r7i.8xlarge offers a dense memory configuration relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads without the premium of GPU hardware. It is suited for in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, enterprise applications, and medium-scale data processing tasks that fit within its 256 GB memory boundary.
description: Amazon Web Services r7i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. It is configured with 256.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities, with top 25% scores in Geekbench single-core and image processing, alongside strong large-block memory write speeds. While small-model LLM inference performs exceptionally well, large-model inference is a notable bottleneck. This instance is suited for memory-intensive applications, in-memory databases, and web serving workloads that require substantial RAM capacity without the need for local physical storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, suited for in-memory databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and advanced Intel Xeon processors for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8488C at 3.2 GHz
256.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory with 8.0 GB per core
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix operations
Strong single-core performance and large-block memory write speeds
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and web serving
